language: kotlin
exerciseType: 1
---

# --instructions--

In Kotlin, **inheritance** lets a class extend another class. A class must be marked `open` to allow it to be subclassed. Override methods and properties with the `override` keyword.

Create an `open` class `Animal` with a `val` property `name` of type `String` and an `open` method `speak(): String` that returns `"..."`. Then create a class `Dog` that extends `Animal` and overrides `speak()` to return `"Woof"`.

# --before-seed--

```kotlin
// DO NOT EDIT FROM HERE
var _testFailedCount = 0;
var _testCount = 0;
fun tryCatch(assertion: Boolean) {
  _testCount++
    try { 
        if (!assertion) throw Exception()
    }
    catch (e: Throwable) {
        _testFailedCount++
        println("Test Case '--err-t$_testCount--' failed");
  }
};
// DO NOT EDIT UNTIL HERE
fun main() {
```

# --asserts--

`Animal("Cat").speak()` must return `"..."`

```kotlin
    val a = Animal("Cat")
    tryCatch(a.speak() == "...")
```

`Dog("Rex").speak()` must return `"Woof"`

```kotlin
    val d = Dog("Rex")
    tryCatch(d.speak() == "Woof")
```

`Dog("Rex").name` must equal `"Rex"`

```kotlin
    tryCatch(d.name == "Rex")
```

# --after-asserts--

```kotlin
// DO NOT EDIT FROM HERE 
    println("Executed $_testCount tests, with $_testFailedCount failures");
}
// DO NOT EDIT UNTIL HERE
```

# --solutions--

```kotlin
open class Animal(val name: String) {
    open fun speak(): String {
        return "..."
    }
}

class Dog(name: String) : Animal(name) {
    override fun speak(): String {
        return "Woof"
    }
}
```
